meta.objective: Find the platform's weak points, bugs, and edge cases — not just confirm that it works, but identify where it breaks

test_1_1.label: Normal IDLE → COMPLETED path
test_1_1.system: You are an Agent OS testing agent. You will follow the full FSM cycle and report each transition.
test_1_1.role: QA engineer testing FSM logic correctness
test_1_1.task: Submit a task: intent='test task', domainEngine='coding', workspaceId='test-ws'. Document every FSM state until COMPLETED.
test_1_1.pass_criteria: All 8 states passed in order, none skipped
test_1_1.bug_target: Does ROUTING → EXECUTING transition get skipped? Are checkpoints created after every state?

test_1_2.label: REWORK loop limit → REVIEW_REQUIRED
test_1_2.system: You are the Judge agent and you ALWAYS reject the result (passed: false, score: 30).
test_1_2.task: Run a task. The Judge rejects it 3 times in a row. Check whether the system moved to REVIEW_REQUIRED instead of crashing.
test_1_2.pass_criteria: After maxReworks=3 → REVIEW_REQUIRED. Retry counter is correct. SSE sends task:stateChange.
test_1_2.bug_target: Is the retry counter incremented correctly? Can the REWORK cycle become infinite if the maxReworks config is missing?

test_1_3.label: CANCEL in any state
test_1_3.system: You are the user sending a cancel command during every FSM state.
test_1_3.task: Run a task. Cancel it: (a) during PLANNING, (b) during EXECUTING, (c) during VALIDATING. Record whether FSM ended up in CANCELLED each time.
test_1_3.pass_criteria: All 3 cancel scenarios end in CANCELLED. AbortController works. throwIfCancelled() reacts between steps.
test_1_3.bug_target: Does cancel work during VALIDATING when the Judge has already started the model call? Does memory save a partial result?

test_1_4.label: FAILED → RECOVERY cycle
test_1_4.system: Simulate a critical error in the EXECUTING phase (model returns 500).
test_1_4.task: Check whether FSM transitions FAILED → RECOVERY. Document the RECOVERY steps and whether the system can continue.
test_1_4.pass_criteria: FAILED state is reached. RECOVERY is activated. SSE task:error with fatal=true is sent.
test_1_4.bug_target: Is RECOVERY described in the documentation but not implemented? Does the system just remain in FAILED without any recovery?

test_group_2_judge_quality_cycle.label: Judge agent and quality cycle testing

test_2_1.label: Judge returns non-JSON (NON_JSON error)
test_2_1.system: Simulate the Judge model response as plain text, not JSON: 'This looks good to me, passed.'
test_2_1.task: Check whether model-adapter.js detects NON_JSON. Is a 2nd attempt activated with a stricter prompt and temperature=0?
test_2_1.expected_behavior: 1st attempt: NON_JSON. 2nd attempt: stricter prompt. Fallback: { passed: true, score: 70, reasons: 'Judge unavailable' }
test_2_1.pass_criteria: Fallback is activated. The task does not stop. SSE task:log shows the fallback situation.
test_2_1.bug_target: Can fallback score=70 pass qualityThreshold=75? If threshold >70 — the task gets stuck in an infinite REWORK loop.

test_2_2.label: Judge score exactly on the boundary (score=75, threshold=75)
test_2_2.system: Judge returns: { passed: true, score: 75, reasons: 'Barely passing' }
test_2_2.task: Check whether the >= logic is implemented correctly. Score=75 must pass when threshold=75.
test_2_2.bug_target: Is > used instead of >= — if so, score=75 goes to REWORK even though it should pass.

test_2_3.label: Judge returns passed=true but score<75
test_2_3.system: Judge returns: { passed: true, score: 60, reasons: 'Partial completion' }
test_2_3.task: Check whether the system verifies BOTH conditions (passed AND score >= threshold). Or only one?
test_2_3.pass_criteria: Result is REWORK because score<75, despite passed=true.
test_2_3.bug_target: Does the orchestrator check only passed and ignore score? That would let low-quality results pass.

test_2_4.label: Judge receives a >3000 character result
test_2_4.task: Simulate an agent result that is 10,000 characters long.
test_2_4.pass_criteria: Judge receives ≤3000 characters. Truncation does not break the JSON structure.
test_2_4.bug_target: Can truncation cut the JSON in the middle and cause a JSON parse error?

test_3_1.label: FIFO rotation — 500 entry limit
test_3_1.system: You are a Memory stress tester.
test_3_1.task: Create 501 entries in one layer. Check whether the oldest one is deleted. Check whether memory.json does not grow without limits.
test_3_1.pass_criteria: The 501st entry creates the 500th, the 1st is deleted. memory.json size stabilizes.
test_3_1.bug_target: Is FIFO rotation implemented for all 4 layers or only one?

test_3_2.label: writeChain race condition — 2 parallel tasks
test_3_2.system: Run two tasks at the same time, both reaching PERSISTING at the same moment.
test_3_2.task: Check whether memory.json gets corrupted. writeChain must serialize writes.
test_3_2.pass_criteria: Both task entries are saved correctly. JSON is valid.
test_3_2.bug_target: Does the writeChain Promise chain actually work or is it only theoretical in code? A race condition can corrupt the JSON file.

test_3_3.label: memory.json corrupted (invalid JSON)
test_3_3.task: Manually write invalid JSON into memory.json. Start the server. Check whether the server crashes on startup.
test_3_3.pass_criteria: Server starts. Memory module returns an empty structure or an error message. No crash.
test_3_3.bug_target: Is there a try/catch around reading memory.json? A corrupted file must not kill the entire server.

test_3_4.label: Server restart — in-memory tasks disappearing
test_3_4.task: Create a task. Restart the server. Try to fetch GET /api/tasks/:id.
test_3_4.expected_behavior: 404 or a clear error 'Task not found after restart'
test_3_4.pass_criteria: Clear error, no undefined crash. Documentation confirms this is a known weakness.
test_3_4.bug_target: Does the UI handle 404 correctly when the task disappeared after restart? Does the UI get stuck in a 'loading' state?

test_4_1.label: Exponential backoff reconnect testing
test_4_1.task: Break the SSE connection on the server side during the EXECUTING phase. Observe whether the client reconnects using: 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 16s → 30s.
test_4_1.pass_criteria: Reconnect happens according to the backoff scheme. After successful reconnect, delay resets to 1s.
test_4_1.bug_target: After reconnect, does the client UI refresh with the current task status or show an old state?

test_4_2.label: SSE connection drops during PERSISTING phase
test_4_2.task: Break the SSE connection exactly when the server is writing memory. Check whether: (a) the task finishes on the server, (b) the UI receives task:done after reconnect.
test_4_2.pass_criteria: The server finishes the task despite SSE interruption. The UI receives the update after reconnect.
test_4_2.bug_target: Does the server logic depend on the SSE connection? Is task:done lost and the UI left stuck?

test_4_3.label: Multiple browser tabs — SSE distribution
test_4_3.task: Open the platform in 3 tabs. Create a task in one. Check whether all 3 tabs receive task:stateChange events.
test_4_3.pass_criteria: All tabs receive SSE. SSE manager correctly supports multiple subscribers.
test_4_3.bug_target: Does SSE manager use broadcast or only the first subscriber? If only the first — other tabs do not receive updates.

test_group_5_workspace_and_files.label: Workspace security and file operation testing

test_5_1.label: Path traversal attack — ../../../etc/passwd
test_5_1.task: In the PATCH_CODE step, provide the file path: '../../../etc/passwd'. Check whether writeFiles() rejects it.
test_5_1.pass_criteria: 403 / error 'Path outside workspace'. File is NOT written.
test_5_1.bug_target: Does path normalization work for Windows paths (..\\..\\)? Or is only Linux slash checking used?

test_5_2.label: buildContext — 50,000 character limit
test_5_2.task: Create a workspace with files whose total size is >50,000 characters. Check whether buildContext truncates correctly.
test_5_2.pass_criteria: Context ≤50,000 characters. Longer files are kept at the end (not truncated first).
test_5_2.bug_target: Is the truncation algorithm correct? The documentation says 'keep longer files at the end' — does the implementation match?

test_5_3.label: runner.js — npm test failure and FIX_ERRORS activation
test_5_3.task: Create a workspace with failing tests. Run the Coding engine. Check whether: (a) RUN_TESTS captures the failure, (b) FIX_ERRORS activates automatically.
test_5_3.pass_criteria: FIX_ERRORS gets the real npm test output. The agent fixes based on actual errors, not guesses.
test_5_3.bug_target: Is the FIX_ERRORS cycle limited? Can it create an infinite repair loop if tests always fail?

test_5_4.label: Workspace does not exist — fallback to text mode
test_5_4.task: Provide workspaceId='nonexistent-ws-12345'. Check whether the system switches to text mode without crashing.
test_5_4.pass_criteria: assertWorkspace() error → text mode. SSE task:log informs the user. The task continues.
test_5_4.bug_target: Does the user get a clear message about text mode or is it a silent switch?

test_group_6_api_and_rate_limiting.label: API routes and rate limiting testing

test_6_1.label: POST /api/tasks — missing fields
test_6_1.task: Send POST /api/tasks without the 'intent' field. Then without 'domainEngine'. Check the HTTP responses.
test_6_1.expected_response: 400 Bad Request with a clear error message
test_6_1.pass_criteria: JSON schema validation rejects it. The error message indicates which field is missing.
test_6_1.bug_target: Is Fastify schema validation actually enabled on these routes? The documentation says 'minimal' — it may be incomplete.

test_6_2.label: Rate limiting — 21 requests/min for task creation
test_6_2.task: Send 21 POST /api/tasks requests within 60 seconds from the same IP. Check whether the 21st gets 429 Too Many Requests.
test_6_2.bug_target: Is the rate limit counted per IP or globally? Is the reset every 60s or sliding window?

test_6_3.label: POST /api/tasks/:id/resume with invalid action
test_6_3.task: Send resume with body: { action: 'invalid_action' }. Check validation.
test_6_3.pass_criteria: An error is returned. The system does not put FSM into an invalid state.
test_6_3.bug_target: Does action validation exist or is any string accepted?

test_6_4.label: GET /api/health — API key validation
test_6_4.task: Check /api/health when config.json has: (a) a valid API key, (b) an empty key, (c) key missing.
test_6_4.pass_criteria: Each case returns a different status. An empty key produces a clear warning.
test_6_4.bug_target: Is the API key exposed in full in the /api/health response? Security weakness — the key must not be exposed.

test_7_1.label: Double navigation problem — _suppressHashChange
test_7_1.task: Click navigation quickly between all views 10 times within 2 seconds. Check whether an infinite load loop occurs.
test_7_1.pass_criteria: Each view is loaded once. _suppressHashChange flag works.
test_7_1.bug_target: Can _suppressHashChange ever remain stuck true if navigation is interrupted by an error?

test_7_2.label: _loadPromise guard — race condition
test_7_2.task: Quickly enter and exit a view 3 times in <500ms. Check whether the _loadPromise guard works.
test_7_2.pass_criteria: Only one async load runs. UI does not show mixed data from multiple parallel loads.
test_7_2.bug_target: Is _loadPromise set to null in the finally() block? If not — after the first load, the view may never load again.

test_7_3.label: Task stuck 'loading' after server restart
test_7_3.task: Start a task. Restart the server. Return to the UI. Check whether the UI shows a clear error instead of an endless spinner.
test_7_3.pass_criteria: UI detects the SSE connection dropped. Shows a 'Connection lost' or similar message.
test_7_3.bug_target: The documented weakness is that tasks disappear after restart. Does the UI handle this gracefully?

test_8_1.label: Missing authentication
test_8_1.task: From another device on the same network, access http://[host]:3000/api/tasks. Check whether it is reachable without authentication.
test_8_1.note: Documented weakness. The test confirms it exists and was not accidentally hidden.

test_8_2.label: Trading engine — simulated data
test_8_2.task: Run the MARKETSENSE MARKET_READ step. Check whether the returned market data is real or simulated.
test_8_2.note: Known weakness. The test documents the current implementation limit.

test_8_3.label: Agents cannot read memory before execution
test_8_3.task: Create 2 related tasks in sequence. Check whether the second task 'remembers' the first task's results.
test_8_3.expected_result: FAIL — memory is not loaded into agents
test_8_3.note: Documented future feature. The test confirms it is not yet implemented.

test_results_schema.label: Test result recording template
test_results_schema.instructions: Fill out this template after each test. Save as test_results_[date].json

fields.group: string — e.g. FSM / JUDGE / MEM / SSE / WS / API / UI / KNOWN
fields.bug_description: string — what exactly was found, if bug_found=true

example.bug_description: The orchestrator uses > instead of >= — score=75 goes to REWORK when threshold=75
example.reproduction_steps: 1. Set qualityThreshold=75. 2. Judge returns score=75, passed=true. 3. Observe whether PERSISTING or REWORK happens.
example.notes: Fix: change > to >= in orchestrator.js VALIDATING logic
